

========== 01_pois_recurring ==========

﻿Recurring Points Of Interest in DLDS

Creation

asdf

---

Soul Plane

asdf

---

The Endless

The Endless is a place between realities that resides in the sea called Creation. In The Endless, only mortals who possess soul protectors can exist. The Endless is ever expanding, and new realities are born every cycle (every 100 Earth years).

The Endless has two extremities, like a type of heaven and hell, divided into layers. Each layer represents the deeds you commit throughout your life.

The layers consist of an infinite number of realities, all the way from -99 to +99. The positive layers are Peak Divinity, and the negatives are a Lovecraftian Nightmare. Souls inhabit these different layers of realities. After death, your soul either ascends or descends in Creation. At the extreme ends of the layers, Gods exist in each reality. They have the power to destroy realities.

A powerful relic of civilization called the Centurion Guard inhabits The Endless. This group are powerful and death-worshipers. Many consider the Centurion Guard to have been the first-ever civilization in Creation. The Guard protects The Endless from ancient threats.

The majority of the inhabitants of The Endless are supernatural creatures of all shapes and sizes. The most prevalent ones are the Reapers. Each reality has a “Grim Reaper” who resides. They determine which souls ascend or descend.

---

The Real World

asdf

---

The Garden Of Creation

asdf

---

Zero Point

asdf

---

Home Realm

asdf

---

Trigon Trading Hub

(There is more than one trading hub, but this one is the most popular hub)
-Is a fragment of Ventus One, that acts as an all-around economic trading world for the various races and species within the Endless.
-The Trading Hub consists of cities that are filled with shops, vendors and wares of all commodities.
-All activities and wares of legal and illegal services with areas and levels going deep.
-It is located within the Centurion Guards territory.

* Locations 
   * Strewn throughout the Endless as with other trading hubs, the Trigon Trading Hub is the biggest and central point for all trade. 
   * They are generally a mix of people from the realities that are with 2-3 levels of the trading hub location 
      * It is rare to see people between 5-10 levels out
      * It is impossible to see those that are more than 15 out, due to the soul resistance
   * What kinds of people will you find
      * The most advanced technology societies are often here making trades
      * There is a lot of mix between races of different realities
      * Those who live at these trading posts are those who ship broke down or they were robbed by endless pirates type thing 
      * Half of the people live on the hub and half travel between
* The main trading hub is where you were find the most variety of people and objects
Abandon Hubs 
   * Are sad and sorry sights to behold
   * Most are abandoned due to disease, war or from a lack of people traveling to that level
* Hubs in the upper and mid level floors are most traveled those in the lower levels are mostly abandoned, There you find desperate souls who have little not resources
* Important note these hubs have no natural resources since most of them are mad on fragments of the Ventus one or similar ship shards, they depend on trading
* Trading between worlds is mostly done to bring in new technology
Added Line Breaks and changed main heading to Title
Focus here
That is a shield imediator panel
Ventus is longer than jupiter
Mary, i added this piece of info as the Trigon Trading hub is the center of commerce for intergalactic trade.
Think deep sea fish when you bring them up to the surface the explode 

except this is your soul 

I'll add this to the soul section of the dictionary but pretty much your soul can't with stand the "energy force" idk what the would be for it of levels well outside of your own.

Only immortal being can with stand it with some discomfort.

Although if your soul is "deserving" Based on your current did in this life you may be able to go beyond it.
Mary this is good, reminds me of Hive Cities in Warhammer, we can bring in gangs, turfs and all manner of seedy or smuggled trading with a black market in this area


========== 02_reapers ==========

﻿Reapers: 
   * Respected by people for their neutral works for passing on soul to salvation or damnation
   * Reapers are distinguished in appearance by Scythes, black cloaks, no faces, they have glowing symbols on their faces.
   * A Reaper that is born is given the duty to reap souls within their reality and defend against intruders from getting into their reality.
   * Don’t like working with mortals, due to a distinction of the Reaper’s work not interfering with mortal lives.
   * Dead realities most have an Intact reaper 
   * If a reaper dies and their reality still exists the said reality will no longer be able to have souls pass on and are infinitely stuck in a slow dying state.Soul Parasites(like tapeworms) will build up and eventually at a breaking point will tear the Reality apart.
   * Reaper weapons have massive slash effects and can teleport the user by pulling them into a directed slash and teleporting them to their destinations in range of sight and not from memory.They draw their power from Creation.
   * Reaper powers usually don’t have cooldowns but fatigue can set in per use of their powers as limit..
   * Teleportation by a Reaper is an exceptional and unique skill. Every reaper has a unique set of skills.
   * Reaper weapons are meant for directing souls to their destinations, not for killing living beings. Scythes when changed into reaper weapons can kill.
   * More powerful reapers can simply touch a mortal or soul to pass it on.(the idea of a gentle hand)
   * Reapers in the Endless are usually looked down on by other supernatural beings for being outdated or traditional values.
   * Reaper’s power or authority is based on the level of their reality, the higher the number the greater their power.
   * Reapers do not require Soul Protectors 
   * Reapers do not have Souls

Reaper Law:
   * Reaper Law states that Reapers are forbidden from reaping any soul except ones they are contracted for. 
   * A reaper is contracted to a soul, if the soul needs to move on or not within their assigned reality except
   * Reapers can reap within the Endless as much as they need/want to. But they must use their preferred tool of the trade.
   * No emotion is to be shown by a Reaper. A soul that becomes a Reaper is void of emotions.
   * All Reapers must help a soul to The Passing but must first use their unique ability to determine where that soul is meant to go. 

                  Above all,
   * Reapers are responsible for sending off Demons or creatures borne of a dark nature in the Endless(Hard Lore) should they escape death. 
* Each Reaper is chosen by Death as the firstborn of their reality, and their abilities reflect their origin species. 
   * Scythes are weapons in the Endless, but less effective in Realities. 
   * Should a mortal touch a Reaper's Scythe, the Reaper is either paralyzed or lose their form or powers.
   * Should anyone impersonate a Reaper they create a Soul Contract as penance which can only be broken through death either by the Reaper impersonated or by the one who formed the contract. 
* Should a Reaper perish, their Reality will have no means of souls passing on.

---

The Reaper–Soul Relationship
Reapers are custodians of the soul cycle.
Their duties include:
1. Harvesting Soul Seeds

2. Sorting souls to their proper layer of Creation

3. Preventing lost or corrupted souls from destabilizing realities

Each Reality is assigned a Reaper to maintain this flow of souls and preserve balance.
Without Reapers, souls can become lost, infected, or weaponized, leading to instability across realities.

---

Souls as Essence
A soul is the condensed record of a mortal life.
It contains:
   * Memory echoes of a life lived

   * The moral weight of one's actions

   * The spiritual energy produced by that existence

When a mortal dies, this essence condenses into a Soul Seed, which becomes visible within the Endless and can be harvested by Reapers.

---

Souls as Currency
Within the Endless, souls also function as the primary form of currency.
This is because soul seeds contain pure existential energy, making them valuable for many purposes.
They can be traded for:
      * Rare relics

      * Magical artifacts

      * Services between supernatural factions

      * Access to restricted locations or knowledge

For example, Reapers may trade large quantities of Soul Seeds at places such as interdimensional trading hubs.

---

Why Reapers Collect So Many Souls
Reapers are sometimes tasked with gathering massive quantities of soul seeds.
This serves several purposes:
         * Maintaining the balance of Creation

         * Powering cosmic systems

         * Trading for artifacts or knowledge necessary for maintaining realities

A Reaper’s ability is often judged by how efficiently they can collect and manage souls.

---

The Danger of Souls
Because souls contain concentrated existence energy, they are highly sought after.
In the wrong hands, they can be used to:
            * Fuel dark rituals

            * Power destructive entities

            * Corrupt entire realities

For this reason, the harvesting and management of souls is tightly tied to the authority of Death and the Reapers.

---

Soul Contract

A Soul Contract is a binding metaphysical agreement in which a soul — or a portion of its essence — is pledged in exchange for power, protection, service, or knowledge.
Unlike ordinary agreements, a Soul Contract is enforced by the fundamental laws of Creation. Once sealed, it cannot be broken without severe consequences to the soul involved.

---

How Soul Contracts Work
A Soul Contract typically involves three elements:
1. The Soul
The essence of a being used as collateral or payment.
2. The Terms
The conditions that define what is being exchanged and the obligations of each party.
3. The Binding
A supernatural seal that ties the contract to the soul itself rather than to physical law.
Once bound, the contract becomes part of the soul’s record.

---

Common Uses of Soul Contracts
Throughout the Endless, Soul Contracts are used for many purposes:
               * Power exchange – gaining abilities beyond natural limits

               * Protection pacts – binding guardians or entities to protect someone

               * Servitude agreements – binding a soul to serve another entity

               * Knowledge transactions – exchanging souls for forbidden knowledge

               * Reality access – gaining passage between restricted layers or worlds

Because souls contain immense existential energy, they are one of the most valuable things a being can offer.

---

The Role of Reapers with Soul Contracts
Reapers generally do not create Soul Contracts, but they are responsible for recognizing and enforcing them when a contracted soul enters the cycle of death.
If a soul is bound by contract, a Reaper cannot simply process it normally. The soul must instead be delivered according to the contract’s terms.
This ensures that the cosmic order of agreements remains intact.

---

The Risk of Soul Contracts
Soul Contracts are dangerous because they permanently affect the fate of the soul.
Possible consequences include:
                  * Eternal servitude

                  * Loss of autonomy after death

                  * Transformation into another type of entity

                  * Being consumed as energy

Some contracts are so powerful they can even alter the destiny of entire realities.

---

The Reaper’s Scythe

-The Scythe has a couple of forms. For self-defense as its main purpose for reaping souls.
-If the reaper does not use a scythe and leaves the soul at its state the soul remains.
-Every Reaper has a unique Scythe design, the design is based off of one’s soul when they were mortal.
-For Firstborn Reapers of Zero-Point Reality they have a scythe based off of Death

---

Reaper Vessel Archive
How are they identified? A few of the characters distinctly wear black, have scythes but seem to have no standardization in terms of uniform. Any other indicators for others to know who they are? Or do they choose not to?
Define people as a collective group? Are all worlds, realities and people know and respect the Reapers as a group?

Why enemies if all people respect the Reapers?
The act needs a different meaning as they do send souls to one side or the other.

I am aware that they have the job to guide souls to these places.
Let's define these locations if there is salvation or damnation.
Define a dead reality to this written law: 

"Should a Reaper perish, their Reality will have no means of souls passing on."
Or their Scythe?
I get it in a means to escape or reposition oneself. 

What are the limits to this teleportation, where does it draw its power? Is it a one time spell that needs recharging? Lets define this further.
No hold time for judgement? salvation or damnation by direct will?
there are exceptions to the rule possibly? What happens if this does happen.
Laws are in Place, what of Reapers that break these rules? Oathbreakers? Ex-Reapers? Loss of Powers? Death?
Let's define this law as to its purpose.
why? as to be impartial?
why would anyone impersonate a Reaper?
Shapeshifters only 

Shapeshifter- become bonded their soul a little
Does this mean that another Reaper can create a Soul Contract for Person A to impersonate another Reaper?

This poses additional questions that may be a plot hole as to situationals
The reaper must have an, underling? deputy? apprentice?
Let's add lore for this specific item, its purpose as a tool, abilities and limitations


========== 03_overview ==========

﻿DIMENSION LOCK — LORE Overview
PURPOSE
This document defines the cosmology, systems, factions, and realities of Dimension Lock. It is a canon-reference for all writers and artists.

---

COSMOLOGY
Creation
* Creation is artificial, not divine
* Composed of infinite realities
* Controlled by layered systems
* No new gods may emerge
Levels
* 99 Positive Levels / 99 Negative Levels
* Greater distance from Neutral = greater soul strain
The Endless
* Interstitial space between realities
* Souls degrade here without protection

---

SOUL SYSTEM
Souls
* Built from emotional components
* Emotional destruction = soul collapse
* Souls rebirth higher/lower based on impact
Soul Seeds
* Byproduct of soul degradation
* Used as fuel, currency, and sustenance
Soul Protectors
* Prevent soul decay in the Endless
* Necklace/emblem form
* Sourced via black market or Centurion Guard

---

REAPER SYSTEM
Reapers
* Appointed by Death
* One per living reality
* If a Reaper dies, their reality stagnates
Reaper Weapons
* Designed to guide souls, not kill mortals
* More effective in the Endless than realities

---

MAJOR FACTIONS
Death Faction
* Death
* Reapers
* Cryious
Centurion Guard
* Alpha Centurion
* Operators (Admin-level)
* Soldiers
Tenebris / Leviathan
* Corruptive force
* Watchers, Runners, Sentries

---

REALITIES (SELECT)
Reaper Ship
* Larger on the inside
* Respawn point for Reapers
* Powered by Soul Seeds
Centurion Reality
* Zero-point origin
* Gothic sci-fi
* Hyper-populated
L-57 (Leviticus)
* Level -30
* Cyberpunk
* Artificial ecosystem

---


========== 04_power_system ==========

﻿DimensionLock Power System
1. The Four Sources of Power
All abilities in DimensionLock come from four fundamental origins.
1. Biological Power
Source: Species, mutation, or environmental adaptation.
These abilities are innate to a character’s body.
Examples:
* Demon physiology

* Creature abilities like Watchers and Runners

* Alien species traits

Characteristics:
   * Cannot easily be removed

   * Often tied to physical advantages

   * Limited by the creature’s biology

Example in story:
Watchers and Runners appear as naturally aggressive creatures rather than magic users.

---

2. Technological Power
Source: Devices, relics, or engineered tools.
Technology can replicate or amplify supernatural effects.
Examples:
      * Seedvault devices that store soul seeds

      * dimensional storage belts

      * AI entities like AI Madoria

Technology allows weaker beings to compete with stronger ones.
Example:
Seedvault machines increase a collector’s capacity to hold soul seeds.

---

3. Spiritual Power
Source: The Endless, souls, and the layer of reality someone exists in.
This is the main supernatural power system.
Spiritual power comes from:
• soul energy
• connection to the Endless
• alignment with reality layers
Every soul sits on a moral scale from -99 to +99.
Categories include:
Rank
	Description
	Commoner
	Minimal impact on the world
	Major
	Significant influence
	Omega
	World-changing individuals
	The stronger the soul, the more energy it contains.
This is why reapers collect soul seeds.
Example:
May is tasked with collecting 10,000 soul seeds.
Soul seeds act like:
         * energy

         * currency

         * fuel

---

4. Administrative Power
Source: Authority over reality itself.
This is the highest tier of power.
Users of Admin abilities can:
            * alter realities

            * purge souls

            * destroy entities instantly

            * manipulate the Endless

Example:
Death destroys entire hordes instantly with a command.
Admin powers belong to beings like:
               * Death

               * high-tier Reapers

               * gods

               * ancient entities

---

Power Scaling System
In DimensionLock, power increases through three axes simultaneously.

---

1. Reality Layer Scaling
Each reality exists between -99 and +99 layers.
                  * Positive layers → divine realities

                  * Negative layers → nightmare realities

Higher layers = stronger existence.
Characters native to higher layers naturally have more power.

---

2. Soul Density
The more souls a being controls or absorbs, the stronger they become.
Soul energy can:
                     * power weapons

                     * fuel spells

                     * increase durability

                     * act as currency

This is why trading hubs exist for soul seeds.
3. Authority Level
Certain roles grant cosmic authority.
Hierarchy example:
Creatures
Demons
Reapers
High Reapers
Death
Gods
Authority determines what rules you can override.
The Power Framework of Creation
All power in Creation derives from four fundamental forces.
These are not just categories of abilities — they are forces that shape existence itself.
Force
	Domain
	Description
	Biological
	Life
	Power originating from species and physiology
	Technological
	Civilization
	Power created through invention or artificial systems
	Spiritual
	Souls
	Power derived from soul energy and the Endless
	Administrative
	Authority
	Power granted by control over realities
	Every character’s abilities are a combination of these forces.

---

Law 1: The Law of Soul Energy
Souls are the fundamental energy of Creation.
Soul Seeds are condensed forms of this energy harvested by reapers.
They function as:
• currency
• power sources
• magical catalysts
• fuel for artifacts
Soul Density
Each soul has an impact level that determines its energy output.
Rank
	Impact
	Commoner
	minimal
	Major
	significant
	Omega
	reality-shaping
	Omega souls are extremely rare and contain vast energy.
This system explains why:
                        * Reapers harvest souls

                        * Trading hubs exist

                        * Soul Seeds power artifacts

---

Law 2: The Law of Reality Layers
Creation is structured vertically across layers from -99 to +99.
These layers determine the maximum power that can exist within a reality.
Positive Layers
Closer to Peak Divinity
Characteristics:
                           * stable realities

                           * higher spiritual density

                           * divine entities

Negative Layers
Closer to Lovecraftian Nightmare
Characteristics:
                              * unstable physics

                              * corrupted souls

                              * monstrous lifeforms

A being’s strength increases when operating in a layer closer to its native power level.

---

Law 3: The Law of Authority
Some entities possess authority over reality systems.
Authority allows a being to override natural rules.
Examples:
Rank
	Authority
	Mortals
	none
	Demons
	limited
	Reapers
	soul authority
	High Reapers
	reality administration
	Death
	universal authority
	Gods
	creation authority
	This explains why Death can erase enemies instantly.

---

Law 4: The Law of Resonance
Objects absorb power from their wielder over time.
Items carried long enough become resonant artifacts.
Example:
Flybutt explains that items owned by a reaper slowly absorb their power.
Resonance explains:
                                 * magical weapons

                                 * relics

                                 * powerful heirlooms

Examples:
                                    * May’s scythe

                                    * her hat

                                    * her necklace

---

Law 5: The Law of Corruption
When soul energy is corrupted, it becomes Madness.
Madness originates from the entity Tenebris and spreads like a cosmic virus.
Effects:
                                       * reality decay

                                       * empty soul husks

                                       * parasitic creatures

                                       * corrupted reapers

Example:
Death finds souls that have become empty shells.

---

The Reaper Power System
Reapers use Spiritual + Administrative power.
Their abilities include:
Soul Reaping
Separating a soul from a body.
Soul Direction
Sending souls to the correct layer.
Soul Collection
Harvesting soul seeds.
Reality Cleansing
Purging infected realities.
Example:
May performs a ritual that pulls lost souls into a portal for purification.

---

Reaper Equipment
Reaper tools are soul-bound artifacts.
Scythes
Primary tool for:
                                          * severing souls

                                          * channeling spiritual energy

                                          * combat

Soul Bags
Contain harvested soul seeds.
Reaper Ships
Travel between realities.
The Power Formula
A character’s power can be described with a simple formula.
Power =
Soul Density
× Reality Layer Compatibility
× Authority Level
+ Biological Traits
+ Technological Augmentation

---

Optional Tier System (Recommended)
You could classify characters like this:
Tier
	Example
	Tier 1
	mortals
	Tier 2
	enhanced beings
	Tier 3
	demons / monsters
	Tier 4
	reapers
	Tier 5
	high reapers
	Tier 6
	Death
	Tier 7
	gods


========== 05_doc_OwDqV ==========

﻿DIMENSONLOCK DEATHLY STORIES

Faction Details

* Operators initiative
* Centorian Guard
* Trigon security 
* Dimensionlock Corp
* Watrari Rebels
* Soul Collectors
* Reapers
* The unholy Ones"vampires"
* Followers Of the abyss 
* Followers of the One who watches
* Leviathans Voice 
* Reaper Hunters 
* Elderions
* Curse bringer
* Muggai Nomads 
* Magic whisperers
* Centura News Corp
* Raiders Guild
* The Supremes Finest 
* The parasites of Realities


========== 06_doc_tuS0q ==========

﻿zDimension Lock Manuscript Plot/Outline
Previous doc
Link to drive (general)
Link to drive (scripts)
Link to Questions and Clarifications

Title:
	Dimension Lock
	Subtitle:
	Deadly Stories
	

VailedLight Entertainment LLC
DimensionLock | English | GlobalComix
http://www.dimensionlock.online
Webtoons
Oregon, USA

Overview

Who are the authors?

Knowing a little about the author will help you achieve the correct tone and style for the book. You can include links to your other content or provide designs to keep in mind when writing the manuscript.

Creator JD: The man with the plan. Our lead writer, the mind behind the story.

Ashe: 

Marvin: Scriptwriter, with a degree in scriptwriting backing them. They love horror and fantasy.

Jordan: 

Mary: 

RJS: 

Omri: Our script editor. An experienced author turned freelancer who had enough of working alone.

	

About the Competition

Is there anything to focus on or look out for that can make this manuscript better than that of your competitors?

	

What is the manuscript about?

Describe or recommend your book in 1 sentence.

As a reaper-in-training, Maytradalis travels the space in-between realities called the Endless. She and her best buddy Flybutt, a very interesting fly, and her great master, a living skeleton, Death will embark on a very challenging journey to find a way to defeat the mysterious entity called the Lurker. Find out how she slowly unveils the mysteries of Creation and the secrets that lie within!
	

What is the manuscript’s positioning?

A fiction book looks something like this: My manuscript targets [audience/ideal reader] and it has elements of [book topics]. The goal of the manuscript is to leave the reader [what you want them to feel/think afterward].

Our manuscript targets young adults and adults, and it has elements of good over evil. The goal of the manuscript is to leave the reader satisfied and have them think about the inevitability of evil in every world.
	

What do you want the readers to remember?

…About the manuscript, and/or what should resonate with them the most?

We want the readers to remember that there is always evil where there is good, but good often prevails.

1. Learning to move on. 
2. Learning to forgive oneself.
3. Finding what it means to be good in a world that's infinitely evil.
4. Cherish the things that are close to us.
	

Target Audience

Mark with X the Target of your book. Choose as many as apply.

Toddler

Present

No

Type of narrator?

Third person

	Other (Please elaborate in the text box.)
	 
	 
	X
	1. Learning to move on. 
2. Learning to forgive oneself.
3. Finding what it means to be good in a world that's infinitely evil.
4. Cherish the things that are close to us.

	 
	 
	

Main Characters
 
May’tra’dalis

General History
May'tra'dalis (stylized as May and Maytradalis) is our protagonist. May is a Half-Reaper (the only Reaper type with no Reality) in training. She is originally Death’s Raven (an all-seeing-eye), appointing Reapers throughout creation. May runs Death’s Vault as an accountant. She is always aiming to become the best Reaper possible. Death created May to replace Sidney’s soul as the only way to defeat the Maddness.

Physical Appearance
Hair Color: Purple with black highlights
Eye Color: Dark gray eyes with bright green irises
Height: 5’7”
Weight: Athletic build; 90-110 lbs.

Personality Traits
Personality: May is hot-tempered. She doesn’t have remorse or care for most others. She is only emotional towards those who are exceptionally close to her. May shows leadership qualities, but she still has a long way to go. She has lost all her emotions and fakes them to seem normal.
Personality Arc: May learns how to be a good person as she studies science. She slowly becomes open to learning how to be in touch with her emotions. May’s arc one plot here.
Flaws: May is a firecracker and gets upset easily. She won't show her emotions right away. May is often forgetful.
Fight Style: Aggressive, acrobatic, not elegant.
Likes: Jazz music, macaroni, tranquillity, birds.
Dislikes: Law-breakers, loud sounds, disruption of order.
Quirks: Hyper-aggressive, sadist.
Trivia: Maytradalis is good with money management and acts as Death’s treasurer/accountant.

Goals
Insert here

Internal Conflict
Insert here

Internal Stakes
Insert here
	

Flybutt

General History
A winged familiar of Matrydalis who acts as a guide, alarm clock, companion, conscience and confidant. He and Matrydalis share a sibling bond. He's a failed attempt at bringing Maytrydalis’(Sydney’s) father back as a combination of souls and now acts as a punishment by Death to accompany Maytridalis for all of eternity.
May created him by accident after trying to recreate her father. The two have been inseparable ever since.
The relationship was awkward in the beginning. Flybutt was just a fly, always in the way. And if you ask May, that’s still how she feels, but the two grew to work together.

Physical Appearance
Hair Color: None
Eye Color: Big eyes with black pupils
Height: 1ft 1 inch
Weight: ~3lbs
Size: Currently not consistent

Personality Traits
Personality: Cautious, kind, wise, anxious, easily frightened 
Personality Arc: He becomes more brave as the story goes on
Flaws: Acts foolishly, sporadic.
Fight Style: Acts as May’s force field
Preferences: Soul Seeds (as a connoisseur), music, reading books
Quirks: Turns to stone (temporarily) when overstimulated or scared shitless.

Goals
Insert here

Internal Conflict
Insert here

Internal Stakes
Insert here
	

Vivian (Gabriel Forge)

General History
Vivian is an inventor. He decides to find who or what killed his reality by infiltrating Death’s Ship. He respects Reapers and wishes to find out how the Reaper of his reality went missing. Losing his family drove him to the current journey of finding out the truth as the last of his kind.
1 million years ago, the Leviathan Tengual destroyed his Reality. He wandered the world for a while. His father promised him he would teach Gabriel the ways of growing the Rasker Flower, but dies before he can entrust Gabriel to carry on his legacy. Gabriel goes on a journey to find the truth of the Leviathan, using his mother's name Vivian (in honor of her memory), so he becomes an inventor and explorer of the Endless.
He makes his way to the Statue of the Leviathan, scared and surprised, but moves forward through the portal, determined to find a way out.
He finds May's room and determines that she must be of importance. He shape-shifts into her but fails to mimic her voice. Flybutt busts him. Vivian tries to run as fast as he can off the ship.
When all efforts fail, and he is about to die, May enacts a Soul Contract with Vivian due to him wearing her hat. He now has to prove his worth, or else he dies.

Physical Appearance
Hair Color: Grayish-brown
Eye Color: Silver
Height: 5’1”
Weight: Stocky build, muscular, ~170lbs

Personality Traits
Personality: Vivian does what he wants. He has a strong sense of right and wrong and doesn’t see much gray. Vivian may do wrong, but he wouldn’t try to excuse his actions. He holds some respect for people above him and has kindness for those under him. Vivian wants to help people and can be quick to form friendships with people. He relies on the natural skills/powers he has gained. He is curious and explorative. Hot-headed and always looking for a fight.
Personality Arc: He calms down with time.
Flaws: Impulsive, arrogant, lazy
Fight Style: Swordsman
Preferences: Inventing
Quirks: Lone Wolf

Goals
Insert here

Internal Conflict
Insert here

Internal Stakes
Insert here
	

Death

General History
Description: Death is the Head Reaper. Death is an administrative system. If he dies, Creation dies and loses its way of appointing Reapers. New Realities cease to function.
Created by Leviathan Protocol 14 to make sure souls got to their location. Death created Reapers dedicated to each Reality. 
Death learned of Prime Realities and took the remaining life of the Home Realm by guiding 50k colony ships to the exit point of their Reality as elsewhere the Madness was born in the 0 Point of Creation
Death is close friends with Cryious.

Physical Appearance
Hair Color: Black
Eye Color: White irises with black pupils
Height: 6”3
Weight: ~160-170lbs

Personality Traits
Personality: Cold and emotionless. He is strict on tradition, policies, and rules. Heath doesn’t give out second chances readily. He has a mostly black-and-white viewpoint as far as what is and isn’t correct. He tries to pretend to be caring but it comes off strange because he’s so flat in voice and his face doesn’t emote.
Personality Arc: He learns to become more emotional and bond with his peers and friends.
Flaws: He has no Soul, He lacks normal means of Reaping due to sacrificing his main ability (the All-seeing Eye/Raven). Anything from the Real World (to a certain extent) can affect him. The Lurker can’t kill Death. Death becomes angry easily.
Fight Style: Old Reaper Form is the Grim Reaper. Uses his Scythe.
Preferences: Crossword Puzzles. Life and its subtle beauty. Love for God (Metrodoria Lightbringer).
Quirks: Meticulous and likes everything to go according to plan.

Goals
Insert here

Internal Conflict
Insert here

Internal Stakes
Insert here
	

Cryious

General History
Cryious is Death’s first reaper born of the 2nd Reality (now non-existent). He is duty-bound as a reaper for Death. Standing as an elite fighter to Death, he currently oversees Maytradalis’ reaper training.
Has been with Death since the beginning as the first Reaperborn.

Physical Appearance
Physical Description: Robed with a Lightning Bolt which signifies the bond he has with Death and the sacrifice he has made for the 2nd Reality. He has no face, just a void where the lightning bolt is.
Hair Color: N/A
Eye Color: N/A
Height: 6”2
Weight: ~160-170lbs

Personality Traits
Personality: Loyal and respectful to Master Death as a brother in arms, dogmatic, and traditional.
In the writing of Death and Cryious, they are mirrors of one another. They can see faults in one another but not in themselves, making the two contradictory.
Personality Arc: He opens up to change with May over time and even at one point accepts that she can be a Reaper and still have emotions.
Flaws: Short temper, stubborn.
Fight Style: Hand-to-hand combat; His mastery of weapons allows him to be a viable mentor to Maytradalis’s fighting style.
Preferences: Lavender, rosemary and the Flower of Death (a flower of pungent/rotten smells)
Quirks: He collects flowers as he believes that they give a glimpse of lives (connected to the gardener of creation).

Goals
Insert here

Internal Conflict
Insert here

Internal Stakes
Insert here
	

Elystria

General History
An anomaly character
Maytradalis chose Elystria to be the Reaper of her Reality and told her that she has to stay away from her family if she wants to be a Reaper.
Elystria walks by a crane that is working on a nearby building. The construction workers are walking back and forth giving the idea of a bustling street. One worker mentioned that the cables are frayed. Another response was that it’s fine, it’s been like that for weeks with no issues.
Meanwhile, Elystria is thinking about the cost of being a Reaper. On the one hand, she is devoted to serving a purpose higher than herself, and this sounds like just that. On the other hand, she loves her husband and daughter and can't bear to lose them. Unfortunately, this moment serves to “distract” the reader from the details of the construction workers. They swerve rather quickly to not hit her, and that is when the comments about the frayed cables are made.
She sees a girl at the park who is by herself and they end up talking. She keeps talking because the girl reminds her of her daughter.
The building's frayed cables snap, and the building collapses on top of the road Elystria’s husband and daughter are on. They were stuck in a traffic jam, and could not escape in time.
Elystria is heartbroken by this happening and even blames herself for their deaths. However, she also blamed Maytradalis for choosing her as a Reaper. 
Over time, she learns the events that happened to her were not really coincidental, but rather the result of her being chosen. This sparks an endless hatred of Death, because she learns that is a law he put into place. But it does eventually give her a chance to forgive May.

Physical Appearance
Insert here

Personality Traits
Personality: A kind soul, she doesn’t run in the face of danger, but that doesn’t mean she isn’t without fear. Of all the Reapers, she displays the most emotion and is the most expressive. 
Personality Arc: She starts to make decisions that aren’t so kind anymore to protect May, showing how far she’s willing to go (even betray her beliefs). She doesn’t want to be a reaper and live forever. Eventually, she dies. She knows who the new gardener is but dies before she can tell anyone.
Fighting Style: Hand-to-hand combat
Flaws: Selfless, Indecisive

Goals
Insert here

Internal Conflict
Insert here

Internal Stakes
Insert here
	

Setting and Worldbuilding

Provide bulleted points about where this story will take place. Include multiple locations‌.

Zero Point
Insert here

Home Realm
Insert here

The Trading Hub
* Is a fragment of Ventus One that acts as an all-around economic trading world for the various races and species within the Endless.
* The Trading Hub consists of cities that are filled with shops, vendors and wares of all commodities.
* All activities and wares of legal and illegal services with areas and levels going deep.
-It is located within the Centurion Guards territory.

	

Story idea in a few paragraphs

What do you envision for this story, within the tropes you’ve chosen? Focus on twists, and turns, and not being predictable. Provide beginning, middle, and end points. What is the first chapter hook going to be?

	

Events list

Where does the story begin, and what events move it along? Include plot points, for the beginning, middle, and end of the story.

List of supporting characters with brief descriptions and why they exist in the story (protagonist, antagonist, etc.)

Manuscript Outline (Chapter Summaries)
ARC 1
Table of Contents

Introduction /Prologue/Foreword: Cat and Mouse/Obsidian
	The backstory of Metrodoria and her soldiers fighting the
Tenebris and its fleets. Metrodoria is killed by the Tenebris while the Vestus One containing her daughter, Medoria (aka Madison), escapes. The operators bring up the Obsidian as a last resort to having the monster locked in its reality. After the news of Metrodoria’s death, Madison is given leadership, in which she can’t handle the pressure. The Tenebris catches up to the Vestus One as Madison comes up with a solution. She sacrifices herself as she bursts into light particles that fill the room and certain people. The failsafe is activated as Operator Chester
sacrifices himself. A light illuminates from the Vestus One and causes the dark space to become a white light. Everyone is disintegrated, and pieces of the Obsidian make it into the black hole. Finally, on an unknown planet, Sydney and her family are introduced as a crow descends into Death’s cage
	Chapter 1: Sound of Devotion
	Introduces Maytradalis and Flybutt and their dynamic.
	Chapter 2: A Reaper’s Banter
	Introduces Death’s ship, and the disappearance of Reapers.
	Chapter 3: The Leap
	Introduces Cryious, Death, along with the lore behind soul seeds and hints of a mysterious malfunction of the scythe. May collects soul seeds.

	Chapter 4: Lurking About
	May fights a frog demon who steals her hat. Sam is mentioned and watches the fight.
	Chapter 5: Paparrazi
	May meets the Lurker, and they fight and gravely injure May. However, May is miraculously healed by the magical pouches, but Flybutt is left unconscious. May makes a run for it back to Death. However, she accidentally bumps into Vivian once again. As the ship departs, Death realizes he must have a fix soon. Vivian sneaks into the ship with his shapeshifting abilities and begins to traverse the endless halls of the ship.
	Chapter 6: Cleansing
	May meets Vivian and forms a Soul contract.
	Chapter 7: Soul Contract
	The main characters for chapter 7 will be May, Vivian, and Flybutt; the mysterious character who May will meet is Sam. It will be very brief; he studies dimensional travel on the planet. Before he leaves, Midoriya AI tells Sam that home is calling, and urgent matters have reached the team’s attention, forcing him to depart. May introduces what reapers can and can’t do to Vivian and trains Vivian on how to catch souls. Four Elite Reapers stand guard, watching everything. May unleashes her more terrifying side in her coaching. Cryious is on a carrier mission in-between to find Omar. Vivian will show off the shifting forms he has collected over time, including the form of a “Demon Destroyer” (can only
be controlled for 10 minutes). The Shadow Demon race created this form, leaving many wondering how Vivian could have acquired it. The Elite Reapers prepare for combat against the Demon Destroyer, but they do not engage. Other than barking orders and critiquing his every move during training, May will be more or less quiet after the talk she had with Sidney, which starts to make her question if she has been too mean. Before she can make a choice, Death intrudes and questions her about it, causing May to later take her hat off on purpose to forget about the hope, repeating. This will further establish the connection between May’s hat and her memories (the longer she is not wearing her hat, the more memories she potentially loses, though she can focus this effect to target certain memories and forget them permanently). Vivian walks alongside Flybutt, and Vivian calls Flybutt “Bloat”. Death and Cryious look onward towards the three outside the ship, Cryious checks out the location that Sam was at but finds nothing to figure out who he is, so Cryious mumbles nothing, strange… All the characters board Death’s ship as they travel to the next destination.
	Chapter 8: Obedience Training
	The main characters will be May, Vivian, Cryious, Kiza, Flybutt, and Izai. May, Vivian and Flybutt will be looking for some supplies for the deeper travels, while Death will be going to the Centurion capital to meet the leader of the Centurion guard. Death asks the chairman for the location of the Obsidian Containment Unit. Meanwhile, May's crew will go back to Death’s ship to stay on guard. Adopted siblings Kiza and Izai will be introduced. Izai is a recruit for the Centurion guard, whereas Kiza is a measly reporter. They will be requesting to stay on the ship. Death and Cryious will permit them, allowing them on the ship. This will surprise May as she is not used to seeing mortals being granted passage on Death’s ship (let alone having rooms for themselves). Vivian will ask for his room, too (“Hey, why don’t I get a room?” type moment), and Death will say, “Sure.” May’s got a basket of fruits, and one of the fruits is ringing, and there will be e a display saying “caller unknown” (the Lurker). Chairmen talk to the shadow person in the corner of the room about having a mission (the Agitator of Sin, established later). End such as having Kiza study and report on reapers onboard Death’s ship. Centurions fully introduced. New Location established.
	Chapter 9: Title
	As the team settles, May will listen to the call. The Lurker taunts May, saying he’s coming. In the background, a statue grotesquely comes to life (Resident Evil style), and stalks toward the party. A Centurion guard insists that May leave focusing an orbital cannon on the statue. A fight ensues between the Centurion guard, the statue, and some involvement from Death himself. The Centurion guards' worship of Death is established here. May has a sudden urge to find the Lurker and fight him. Death has everyone board the ship and depart. Death will surprise Vivian in his room and intimidate him about why he came in the first place onto Death’s ship. This will leave Vivian pondering. Flybutt and
May will talk about the possible whereabouts of the Lurker's next attack. The scene will end with Death’s ship entering the clouds.
	Chapter 10: Title
	The theme of the chapter is gonna be possibly a meanwhile type of setting and will be following Sam. There will be a few scenes dedicated to him. Midoria’s AI. He will find a way to bypass the admin system to find his love. sooner or later he will cross the crossroads with Death’s team. Death is holding a reaper gathering where thousands of reapers congregate. When May shows herself, all the reapers look at May with disdain. He's having a meeting about the obsidian devices and the
ongoing fight against the Lurker, unaware that the Lurker is listening the entire time via the newly formed realities (easier to corrupt with no/newly established reapers). Established here is that when a soul is corrupted, it becomes hidden from Death’s view. If the firstborn soul is tainted before the reality is fully formed, it will always be hidden from Death. This also helps to explain why Sam can retain his memories no matter how many times he dies. While this meeting is going on, Vivian will be taunted by a reaper sitting beside him who is sharpening his blade. Afterward, there will be some staring contest between May and that reaper, and Vivian will feel the heat. May will be called a reaper killer, because of her past self, which catches Vivian off guard but is easily distracted by Flybutt's rambling. The scene ends with the 3 chilling outside. With May having Flybutt sleeping on her hat and May asking Vivian why he hasn’t died yet what was with the girly name and if it was for some kink... Vivian will say it's for family remembrance... May will react with feeling distraught about it saying “I guess I understand family a little... We got a lot of work weirdo... Be ready. Death isn't the one to play chill.” Cryious and Death try to guess the next reality the Lurker will destroy and theorize the next one...
	Chapter 11: Title
	May and Flybutt adventure. A situation occurs where May can do nothing to save a person from dying, as is per reaper law. A child is getting bullied upon a bridge. This causes a conflict within May. She ends up reaping the kid after he falls off the bridge. As she reaps his soul, she lies that his mother is waiting for him. Despite May’s inability to cry (rather, with her keeping her emotions in check, only a single tear runs down her cheek), it is an emotional scene regardless. Flashback will show her training with a reaper on how to reap a soul. She ends up having trouble reaping the soul because her emotions get the better of her. She figured there was still hope, so she went out to find the parents. The child died from starvation, leaving her no choice but to reap his soul right then, or it would
Existence would erase them, they would be left in limbo, or a Soul Collector would gather them.
	Chapter 12: Title
	Kiza backstory. Get to know more about her character and her relationship with her brother Izai. May finds a way to contact the Lurker, threatening him that she will find him. She kept the apple that he used on her last time, and it opened up Pac-Man style to reveal a screen. Upon staring at it for too long, May gets sucked in, where the Lurker puts her through a mini-game world of sorts. The Lurker has already destroyed each reality that May finds as an apple. Vivian and Flybutt. discover what’s going on, inform Death, and he will pull May out of the game and destroy the apple. Cryious will slam May against a wall for being so stupid and falling prey to Lurker’s trap. May taunts Cryious, saying that anger is an emotion, and reapers don’t have emotions. Vivian and Kiza carry off May, and Death will shake his head at Cryious and walk away.
	Chapter 13: Calm Before the Storm
	May helps Death in the Library, and Death finally spills the beans on why he is so worried that May will fail. Death will mention the reason Sidney could be acting up is possibly because someone is trying to breach the Blackbox. The only question is: who? The answer is Sam, but Death and May do not know; only we, the audience, know the truth. May will learn more about the Obsidian and the plan for using it against the Lurker. Death will also share the location for this ultimate mission, and May will be meeting up with the most honored guard, sent by the
Chairman to assist May on her mission (the Agitator of Sin, whose real mission is to analyze May and determine whether or not she is a heretic). May will also learn how dangerous this reality truly is, especially when it comes to emotions. This chapter will conclude with her having a good time with her friends. A picture hangs on the wall, taken before the mission starts, and the chapter concludes with a light-hearted moment. A calm before the storm.
	Chapter 14: Title
	May and her crew team up with the Agitator of Sin to retrieve the Obsidian Containment Unit for Death. They fight off against a corrupted Ragdoll, and Vivian unleashes his Demon Destroyer form. During the fight, May helps up a Centurion guard. And this sends a red flag to the Agitator of Sin, giving him the proof he needs. When the Agitator orders the guards to shoot down May as she is running to find Vivian, Kiza initiates a fight. The Agitator of Sin overrides Kiza’s suit, turning it against her and gaining control. May returns to see the situation, but is confused and doesn’t know what exactly is happening. May inadvertently kills Kiza because she does not realize Kiza’s suit is being controlled by the Agitator of Sin. During this entire fight, Kiza is screaming and begging to be let go. Even as she is dying, she continues to beg to be let out of her suit. When May finally realizes what has happened, distraught by the fact that she killed a friend, the Agitator of Sin calls May out as a heretic. Before she has a chance to do anything, he fires upon her. The Agitator of Sin blasts May, tearing off her limbs and disintegrating her body until very little remains. The Agitator of Sin, in his Demon Destroyer form, swiftly overwhelms Vivian, showing how outmatched he is. Flybutt convinces Vivian to run, and they flee in terror knowing they can do nothing. May is not dead, but the Blackbox is weakened. In that moment, Sam realizes he now has the opportunity he’s been waiting for and captures Sydney’s soul from the Blackbox. This reverses the two, leaving Sydney on the surface and May trapped in the Blackbox.
	Chapter 15: Title
	Sam and his crew protect Sidney from continuous threats. All the while, Vivian and Flybutt begin a rescue mission to find and retrieve May, not knowing that. May is currently Sydney.
	Chapter 16: Title
	Sam’s crew races around the clock to find a way to save Sidney's soul from returning to the Blackbox. May is tearing down the forest that was once the garden. While she is desperate to get out, she doesn’t know that she is destroying the emotions of Sydney. Sydney begins to flicker in and out of consciousness and the Blackbox. In a last-ditch effort to save Sydney, Sam will get sucked astrally into the dimension the Blackbox resides in. He begins punching at the barrier keeping him out of the Blackbox, but this only causes Sydney to despair. He manages to get his hand through and reaches for Sydney. He glances at The Madness, temporarily driving him insane. Sydney distracts him by taking his hand and looking into his eyes. As she lays her head upon his hand, a single tear runs down her cheek to indicate she has given in. Sydney remains in the Blackbox, allowing May to return to the surface.
	Chapter 17: Return
	The return of Maytridalis, aftermath, Death getting vengeance on the guard for what happened to May (including and especially the Agitator of Sin), Sam going into hiding.
	Chapter 18: Title
	May recovers, notices she doesn't have emotions and will save a flower that remains of Sidney's soul in the Blackbox. Death will debate on reaping her but will decide to allow things to play out. May will receive a new outfit and will have a different look. Flybutt and Vivian will comfort May, and Vivian will question her. The chapter ends with Death preparing a reaper group as he looks onward at the friends and then toward the devices in front of him that form the Obsidian. The Agitator of Sin will return to life via a grotesque scene (he punches his way out of an expecting mother’s womb, forming into a fully grown man before his feet touch the floor). Immediately reporting to the chairman with his debrief. The Lurker appears in an unknown, futuristic city with a basket of apples which he will precede to offer two choices. The person will reach out their hand and the chapter cuts abruptly.
	Chapter 19: Title
	

	Chapter 20: Title

	Tease Cryious working on the garden
	Chapter 90: Eyes of Mine

	Chapter 97: 
	

	Chapter 98: 
	

	Chapter 99: 
	

	Chapter 100: Can We Change
	Vivian asked about her
	FINAL POINT
	Chapter 101: Building Blocks

	Chapter 104: Devious Intentions

	A metaphor for the life cycle and metamorphosis. Different sides of the same coin. May deals with her past self. Talks to Vivian about it. Tease a romantic bond between the two.

Chapter-by-Chapter Summary

Next, you’ll provide more information about the actual chapters in terms of headings, subheadings, information to be included in each, and so forth.

Chapter 1: Title

What do you want the hook for the chapter to be? (1-sentence summary.)

	

Supporting Content

What plot points do you want here? Any points of conflict? Share in more detail what you expect from this chapter.

	**repeat above structure for as many chapters as necessary**

Epilogue/Afterword

What’s the close? Is there a cliffhanger?


========== 07_doc_PWVsa ==========

﻿CONTINUITY FIX TABLE — CHAPTER 5 → CHAPTER 6

---

1.  FULL REALITY PURIFICATION (BIGGEST CONFLICT)
CURRENT CHAPTER 5 READ
	CANON-ALIGNED FIX
	WHY THIS WORKS
	Purification Super Cruisers deploy devices into seven suns that explode and purify the Reality, killing infections and parasites
	Reframe this as Emergency Containment Protocol, not cleansing. Visuals stay large, but results are temporary suppression
	Chapter 6 requires the Reality to still be infected and unstable
	Visual implies the problem is “handled” before May even meets the Lurker
	Add staff-facing intent: Containment buys time, does not cure infection
	Makes May’s later solo action necessary, not reckless
	Contradicts Soul Send being special
	This becomes a failed blunt-force solution
	Soul Send now reads as smarter, not redundant
	Practical Script Adjustment (NO dialogue change)
* Change visual language, not text:

   * Suns glow unstably, not clean

   * Purification symbol fractures

   * Ash drifts but does not resolve husks permanently

✅ Chapter 5 ends with illusion of control
 ✅ Chapter 6 reveals truth of failure

---

2. REAPER STATUS CONFUSION (RODIGUS / ROTAGHAST)
CURRENT CHAPTER 5 READ
	CANON-ALIGNED FIX
	WHY THIS WORKS
	Centurion Guard protects a dying Reaper, implying they are still alive
	The Reaper is already dead or irreversibly gone—guards are protecting the site and remains
	Chapter 6 requires May to discover ashes, scythe intact
	Emperor Medic “finds” the Reaper infected
	Medic confirms post-mortem madness saturation, not survivability
	Prevents contradiction with Rodigus being ash
	Feels like May arrives “too late” redundantly
	May arrives after Death’s forces withdraw, changing stakes
	Preserves tragedy without repetition
	Practical Script Adjustment
      * Keep the scene.

      * Change interpretation:

         * No pulse

         * No dialogue implying recovery

         * Guards speak tactically, not medically

✅ Reaper is lost before Chapter 6 begins
 ✅ May’s discovery becomes confirmation, not retcon

---

3.DEATH AS ACTIVE FIXER VS MAY AS FIELD AUTHORITY
CURRENT CHAPTER 5 READ
	CANON-ALIGNED FIX
	WHY THIS WORKS
	Death annihilates enemies personally
	This is last-resort intervention, not a solution
	Sets a ceiling on Death’s involvement
	Death points at the Lurker, forcing him to pause
	The Lurker halts from recognition, not fear
	Keeps Lurker above brute force
	Death says “We need a permanent fix”
	This is the handoff line to May’s authority
	Makes Chapter 6 logical, not rebellious
	Key Interpretation Shift
Chapter 5 = Death realizes he can’t solve this directly
 Chapter 6 = May acts because someone has to
✅ Death trusts May later
✅ May is not overstepping—she’s filling a vacuum

---

4. MAY’S SOLO DECISION FEELS RECKLESS (IF UNFIXED)
CURRENT READ
	FIXED READ
	WHY IT WORKS
	May storms off emotionally in Chapter 6
	May acts from earned responsibility seeded in Chapter 5
	Guilt + failure → agency
	Flybutt’s injury feels like a plot device
	Flybutt’s injury becomes moral consequence
	Raises emotional stakes
	Elystria objects too late
	Elystria’s objection mirrors audience fear
	Grounds the choice
	Reinforcement (No Rewrite)
            * Emphasize May’s silence at end of Chapter 5

            * Reduce quippiness in final ship scene

            * Let Cryious’s disappointment linger

✅ May’s choice reads as necessary, not impulsive

---

5. LURKER’S ROLE CLARITY (SUBTLE BUT IMPORTANT)
CURRENT READ
	CANON-ALIGNED READ
	WHY IT WORKS
	Lurker feels like the cause
	Lurker is a vector / catalyst
	Chapter 6 introduces madness as systemic
	Obsession with May feels personal
	Obsession = resonance, not blame
	Preserves May’s innocence
	“Madness” feels vague
	Madness is now detected everywhere
	Sam & Madoria payoff
	✅ Lurker becomes symptom and threat
✅ Bigger arc opens instead of collapsing inward

---

FINAL CANON OUTCOME (AFTER FIXES)
By the end of Chapter 5, the audience understands:
✔ The Reality is contained but dying
 ✔ The Reaper is already lost
 ✔ Death cannot fix this alone
 ✔ May feels responsible but capable
 ✔ The Lurker cannot be killed
By the start of Chapter 6, nothing feels retconned—only revealed.


========== 08_doc_zz9Ru ==========

﻿MASTER GUIDE
(TO BE UPDATED REGULARLY)
  

DIMENSIONLOCK/STORY DESIGN
─
PLEASE REFER TO NEW DOC

VailedLight Entertainment LLC
DimensionLock | English | GlobalComix
http://www.dimensionlock.online
OREGON, USA
Overview (Read to Get Started and Catch Up)
As a reaper-in-training, Maytradalis travels the space in-between realities called the Endless. She and her best buddy Flybutt, a very interesting fly and her great master, a living skeleton, Death will embark on a very challenging journey to find a way to defeat the mysterious entity called the Lurker. Find out how she slowly unveils the mysteries of Creation and the secrets that lie within! 

---

Objectives
1. To be able to create a master document for the writing team as a point of reference.
2. To create a wiki point for the audience/community to engage with.
3. To create a pitch deck that discusses the story’s background, characters, themes, and plot lines that will be utilized for the project proposal.
Team Assignments
Formatting Notes
1. Formatting was created to standardize our writing documents, processes, story beats, and elements and avoid overlapping
2. The objective of this DLDS Master Guide is to make sure that we have a coherent story that is organized and well-written.
3. All Story elements will have H1 Headings, H2/H3 Headings are sub-elements hyperlinking to their respective documents.
4. Yellow text is a description. Italicized highlighted text with underlines are staff-related notes.
5. Line Breaks have been added for breaks and will be implemented to other documents for proper formatting.

---

Frequently Asked Questions
Refer to this section on the frequently asked questions and discussions of lore.

---

Mary’s DS: Re-Write Notes
This section uses Mary’s notes on the lore and other tidbits concerning DimensionLock. 
From 10/24 Meeting, its agreed that Arc 1 will be the focus for development.

---

Story Themes
This section emphasizes the core themes centered on DimensionLock. 
1. Learning to move on. 
2. Learning to forgive oneself.
3. Finding what it means to be good in a world that's infinitely evil.
4. Cherish the things that are close to us.

---

Origin Story
Marvin’s Origin Story Doc

---

Refer to Synop Document for Updated Info

1. The Beginning  
120-150 chapter Synopsis est.
2. The Middle
120-150 chapters Synopsis est. 
3. The Climax
60 chapters Synopsis est.

This section provides the completed chapters of the ongoing series.

Refer to this section as a guide for the locations mentioned and are set in DimensionLock.

========== 09_doc_V2dRw ==========

﻿🌀 DimensionLock: The Full Series Bible
Core Concept: A cosmic mystery-thriller set within a resource-extraction simulation (The Creation Engine) on a planetary-ark (Venus) during a deep-space transit to Alpha Centauri in the year 4400.
I. THE HARD LORE: THE REAL WORLD (YEAR 4400)
1. The Venus-Ark Transit
* Chronology: The Year is 4400. The Sun has died.
* The Setting: Planet Venus is the final bastion of humanity, retrofitted as a vessel drifting toward Alpha Centauri.
* The Creation Engine: A massive industrial simulation on Venus's surface. It generates Litenite, an "unlimited material" created from the deaths of stars and the extraction of soul-density within simulated realities.
* Time Dilation: Millions of years pass inside the Engine (The Endless) for every month of real-world transit time on Venus.
2. The Board of Humanity & The Protocols
* The Board: The overarching antagonists. They prioritized human survival over the original altruistic "World Development" goals of the engine.
* The Lovecraftian Protocol: An anti-virus/hard-reset command. It blocks all entry/exit to the Engine and triggers the Tenebris (security sub-routines) to delete threats to the Board’s harvest.
* The Resource Crisis: The Engine was consuming more energy than it generated due to "Soul Load" (inhabitants living too long). The Board shifted the mission to "Extraction."
II. THE SOFT LORE: THE ENDLESS (THE SIMULATION)
1. Perception vs. Reality
To those inside the Engine, the world is a mythological "Endless" of floating realities.
* The Garden of Creation: The Engine's UI. Each Flower is a reality; each Glow inside is a Reaper.
* Death (The Gardener): Worshipped as the God of Balance. In truth, he is the original Administrator who stayed to manage the harvest.
* The Obsidian (DimensionLock): An anti-supernatural bomb that "shutters" a reality, harvesting its matter for Litenite.
III. CHARACTER ARCHITECTURE
1. Maytradalis (The Protagonist)
* Nature: An artificial "Raven-soul" created from Death's sacrificed All-Seeing Raven ability.
* The Scythe: A quarantine chamber for Sidney (May's human soul) and The Madness.
* The Madness: A fragment of original creation logic residing in Sidney’s soul. The Board perceives it as a lethal virus that kills Reapers.
* Evolution: Starts as a manic thrill-seeker (due to Madness leakage). To become a "Full Reaper," she must destroy Sidney's emotions (Love, Joy, Fear, and finally Hope).
* The Identity Hack: May decides to Act on the Memory of her emotions. She fulfills the "Reaper" requirements by being emotionless, but simulates "Goodness" based on archives of Sidney's soul.
2. Madoria Lightbringer (Madison Blair)
* Nature: The daughter of the original Architect, Metrodoria Lightbringer.
* The Centerpiece: While May is the shield/warrior, Madoria is the only one with the "Administrative Bloodline" to override the Board and save the Engine.
3. The First Operator (The Ghost / New Gardener)
* Nature: The original human builder of the Engine.
* Role: Appears as a glitchy "Ghost" in Arc 2. He exists in the metadata outside the boundary. He is the New Gardener waiting to plant humanity in the New Garden once they reach Alpha Centauri.
4. Death & Cryious (The Witnesses)
* Death: The original Gardener who sacrificed his "eyes" to create May.
* Cryious: The First Witness. He "dies" in Arc 1 to cross the boundary and monitor the New Garden with the First Operator.
5. The Agitator of Sin
* Role: Leader of the Harbingers of Light Squad, a Sec Op (Security/Secondary Operations) unit under the Centurion Guard.
* Philosophy: Obsessed with "Sin" (efficiency/individuality). He views May as a corrupted file that must be purged.
IV. FACTIONS
Faction
	Alignment
	Objective
	Board of Humanity
	Antagonist (Boardroom)
	Harvest Litenite; Survive transit at any cost.
	Centurion Guard
	Lawful Neutral (Military)
	Protect the Engine; Worship Death; Purify realities.
	Harbingers of Light
	Sec Op (Elite)
	Hunt individuality and "Sin" within the Reaper ranks.
	The Tenebris
	Automated Security
	Delete anything resembling the Architect's logic.
	Shadow Faction
	Renegade
	Hunt Reapers; Betray the Gardener’s mission.
	The Lurker
	External Anomaly
	Trapped scientist from Venus attempting to break the code.
	V. NARRATIVE ARC ROADMAP
Arc 1: The Manic Harvest
* Setting: Supernatural "Endless."
* Plot: May struggles with the Madness. The Harbingers monitor her.
* Climax: May shuttering her first reality via the DimensionLock. She destroys Hope but vows to "Act on Memory."
* End Reveal: Cryious crosses the boundary.
Arc 2: The Metadata Rebellion
* Setting: Reality "Glitches."
* Plot: Meeting the First Operator (The Ghost). The discovery that "magic" is code.
* Climax: The Harbingers of Light declare May a system corruption. Madoria (Madison Blair) begins to manifest.
Arc 3: The Venusian Transit
* Setting: Hard Sci-Fi (Venus 4400 / Alpha Centauri).
* Plot: The Board triggers the Lovecraftian Protocol for a total wipe.
* Climax: May faces the Agitator in a final Sec Op duel. Madison executes the Root Override.
* Resolution: The New Gardener (First Operator) opens the gates. Souls are transitioned into the real bodies for the new world.
VI. THEMATIC CORE & TERMINOLOGY
* The Weight of Deeds: The algorithm that chooses a Grim Reaper based on their administrative potential.
* Performative Identity: Maytradalis's choice to be "Good" through simulation rather than feeling.
* The Paradox: Humanity must destroy their simulated "children" to survive, but the Architect’s daughter and a Raven-soul choose to save them instead.


========== 10_doc_YBN75 ==========

========== 11_doc_qB696 ==========

﻿Overview Document

Purpose: This document.aims to gain an understanding of the Dimensionlock Comics for its story, characters, and lore bits. 
Introduction
As a reaper-in-training, May'tra'dalis travels the space in-between realities called the Endless. She and her best buddy Flybutt, a very interesting fly and her great master, a living skeleton called Death, will embark on a very challenging journey to find a way to defeat the mysterious entity called the Lurker.
Story Themes
This section uses emphasizes the core themes centered on DimensionLock. 
1. Learning to move on. 
2. Learning to forgive oneself.
3. Finding what it means to be good in a world that's infinitely evil.
4. Cherish the things that are close to us.
Chapter 1 - Sound of Devotion
The Story Begins with a Dream…
Summary
The story introduces us to May'tra'dalis in a dream receiving her Reaper’s Scythe from Death who is her master.Her goal as a Reaper is to bring order to creation which are different realms. As Death’s Maid she is tasked to search for pieces of a device called the Obsidian. 

“In the dream sequence, once the Reaper’s Scythe has been given to May. Death seems to disintegrate into dust as May is struggling against a strong force that swallows her whole.” This seems to be a usual dream as an interaction with Flybutt who has awoken May as she is summoned by Death for her current Reaper task.

It would seem that a lot of Reapers are already gone by the time May and Flybutt have a commentary of the lack of them as they have departed.The story ends on a cliffhanger at Death’s quarters where May wishes to affirm her willingness to prove her worth for today’s job.

Characters
  

May'tra'dalis
(Right) - Our Protagonist
Flybutt
(Left) - May'tra'dalis’ companion
Items

The Obsidian 
A powerful artifact that is to be revealed later in the story.

Reaper's Scythe
An item that is given to May'tra'dalis as part of her trial completion. She is newly appointed as a reaper through the dream.
Locations

Death’s Ship (Name?)
  

May’s Room
  

Ship Interior - Reaper Quarters
  

Interior Location - Entrance to the Surge Hanger
  

Death’s Chamber (Exterior)
  

Chapter 2 - 1.2
May Enters Death’s Office and Receives Her Task.

Summary
We get introduced to Maytrilis’s role as a Reaper during her first day on the job. She is tasked by Death to harvest 10,000 Soul Seeds. She is watched over by a looming figure named Cryious. Maytrilis also meets a friend named Ronane who acts as a mechanic on Death’s ship. She looms over the Endless and the many Realities that lie below to begin her duty.

Characters
May'tra'dalis - 

Flybutt - 

Death - Maytrilis’s Boss who tasks Maytrilis to harvest 10,000

Cryious - A Reaper that is tasked by Death to observe Maytrilis during her first day as a Reaper
  

Ronane - Surge Hangar Mechanic - Friends with May'tra'dalis
  

Items
A Rattling Cage?
  

Soul Seeds Bag
  

Soul Seed (Unreaped) 
  

Locations

Death’s Office

  

Surge Hangar
  

Chapter 3 - 1.3
A Walk Of Faith, May Takes the Leap. 

Summary
May'tra'dalis diligently collects 9,999 Souls, She risks venturing further to catch the last remaining soul that she needs to complete 10,000. The Endless is revealed to be a realm of fog and clouds. May'tra'dalis seems to require focus in order to venture out and walk among the clouds.She almost falls but is narrowly saved by Cryious. Upon presenting all the Souls that Maytrilis has collected. Death nonchalantly assigns the new reaper 20,000 more souls to reap.

This chapter introduces May’s Soul Reaping abilities, the work that Reapers do in the Endless.
Characters
May'tra'dalis & Flybutt
  

Cryious 
  

Death
  

Items

  

Soul Seed, May’s Reaper Scythe, Soul Seed Bag?
Locations
The Endless and the Souls
  

Death’s Ship
  

Death’s Office Interior
  

Trading Hub (Mentioned)

Not defined what this means but the next chapter offers a glimpse.

Chapter 4 - 2.1 
Chapter 2: A Visit Of Importance

Summary
May'tra'dalis shows her skills when reaping the 20,000 souls. A ship passed by and Her Scythe seems to malfunction as she mentions the name Sydney. 
  

Cryious gives a lecture regarding May'tra'dalis’ emotions as the only factor that is holding her back from becoming a full reaper. Time in the Endless is different; It is currently the 100th cycle and May'tra'dalis seems to have forgotten a few lessons from her past.

Death will want to take May'tra'dalis to the Trading Hub; they have a brief talk regarding May's wealth and how “realities cannot be saved by all the Soul Seeds in the Endless”.

While she is on standby. Death and Cryious privately discuss the situation involving May and how her emotions will be a liability to the main mission of hunting for the Obsidian and The Maddness that is uncontrolled may kill them all. Death is determined that May'tra'dalis will fulfill her purpose.

Meanwhile, May'tra'dalis calls up a white robed female named Elystria, a woman located at The City of Laviticus - The Capital of Bones for an update. The chapter ends with a mysterious figure in the presence of Elystria

Characters

Sydney - Briefly Mentioned
  

May'tra'dalis, Flybutt, Death, Cryious in one scene

Elystria - May’s friend who knows of May’s Soul Reaping duties.
  

Items
-None listed to be seen as relevant or of importance possibly.
Locations
The Command Floor
  

A view of the Levels of Realities
  

The Cresent Renlit -53
  

The City of Laviticus - The Capital of Bones

  

Chapter 5 - 2.2 
The Agitator of Sin and his crew rush against time to save a dying reaper but are met with a surprise.

Summary
The scene opens up to a ship dropping at a reality where strange tendrils engulf what seems to be a planet. The ship transports a squad of soldiers to find a targeted Reaper,its untimely death caused by The Lurker, an entity which seems to create and control these Reality-eating parasites.

These soldiers receive intel from a reaper who specializes in facing the Lurker and saving Realities. The A.o.S (Agitator of Sin) would report back to an Alpha Superium King.

This seemingly disconnected intro.actually shows a few plot points within the story that is correlated
-Shows the Agitator of Sin as another faction
-Reveals how a dead reaper can throw things out of balance.
-Reveals reality eating parasites
-Explains how realities die

Meanwhile Death and all aboard Death’s Ship are transported to what we can assume to be the Trading Hub. The comic issue ends with May'tra'dalis and the group seem to be strolling.through the City streets.
Characters

Agitator of Sin - A powerful warrior 
  

A Troll left by the Lurker
  

Alpha Superium King - briefly mentioned

Items
-None seem relevant.
Locations

A view of an ending Reality
  

Inside- The Ship

  

Srandles Reality 
-briefly mentioned as the Reality above TTH

The Trading Hub?
  

Chapter 6 - 2.3
A trade is done and memories are resurfaced.

Summary
The chapter starts with May'tra'dalis wandering through the many stalls with a variety of different wares and races. Slaves,weapons, oddities, flora and fauna are being sold.Death and May'tra'dalis enter a shop owned by Merillious who seems to deal with artifacts/relics of power.

May'tra'dalis is then urged to enter a cage with the different kinds of Lurkers as a trial. May partially transacts the soul seeds to feed the chained creatures but has forgotten her training is cornered and is panicking. It is by Death’s intervention due to his impatience that May escapes and Merilious hands out the Obsidian Husk.

Through the streets of the Trading Hub a doomcaller beckons the crowd with the warnings of the destruction of Realities brought about by a ‘great evil.’ May'tra'dalis is called out and she remembers a dream. A dream with Death and a Raven that flies out of its cage. The comic issue ends with the phrase

‘For my greatest fear, The Maddness, that is in my soul’ is an image showing the Raven’s feathers descending with a figure draped in white.
Characters

Merillious - an antique collector of rare items.
  

The Different Creatures
  

Runners - Primary purpose is to chase down prey for the Lurker.

  

Seekers - Primary Purpose is to keep watch of what the Runners find.

Items
The Obsidian Husk - An artifact of power.
  

A Raven’s cage held by Death
  

Locations

The Trading Hub
  

Merillious’ Shop
  

A Door that leads to?
  

Do redefine how timelines work as she's aged over a Million?
The way this was seen is through a call from May, Reapers have maps of all the levels of the Realities?
Legit spelled as Cresent or Crescent? Also do explain that within this reality is the City of Laviticus? A brief description is vital for understanding.
Is TTH short for The Trading Hub?
